title: When specifying multiple port mappings, only one port gets published
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

When specifying multiple port mappings, only one port gets published.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Set env variables as following:
```shell
DATA_FOLDER=/mnt/resilio_sync
WEBUI_PORT=8888
mkdir -p "$DATA_FOLDER"
```

2. Start a container with the following command:
```shell
podman --runtime /usr/bin/crun run \
       --replace --pull always \
       --label "io.containers.autoupdate=image" \
       --tz 'Asia/Taipei' --cgroups no-conmon \
       --restart=unless-stopped --name Sync \
       -p 127.0.0.1:"$WEBUI_PORT":8888 \
       -p 55555 \
       -v "$DATA_FOLDER":/mnt/sync \
       -d resilio/sync
```

3. Check whether the local port 8888 is listening by a process:
```shell
ss -nl 'sport = :8888'
```
```
Netid                State                Recv-Q                Send-Q                               Local Address:Port                               Peer Address:Port                Process
```

```shell
podman port Sync
```
```
55555/tcp -> 0.0.0.0:26291
```

```shell
podman inspect Sync
```

As above mentioned.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Every port mapping gets published.

comments.body:
A deeper dive turns out that the latest podman seems failed to respect to [the syntax of publishing ports (http://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-create.1.html?highlight=publish#publish-p-port)](http://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-create.1.html?highlight=publish#publish-p-port):

```shell
podman --runtime /usr/bin/crun run \
       --replace --pull always \
       --label "io.containers.autoupdate=image" \
       --tz 'Asia/Taipei' --cgroups no-conmon \
       --restart=unless-stopped --name Sync \
       -p "8888:8888" \
       -p 55555 \
       -v "/mnt/resilio_sync:/mnt/sync" \
       -d resilio/sync

podman port Sync
```

```
+ podman port Sync
8888/tcp -> 0.0.0.0:12385
55555/tcp -> 0.0.0.0:13589
```

We recently merged some fixes to how ports are parsed. Can you try again
against the later commit?

On Wed, Dec 16, 2020 at 03:45 IceCodeNew <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> A deeper dive turns out that the latest podman seems failed to respect to the
> syntax of publishing ports
> (http://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-create.1.html?highlight=publish#publish-p-port)
> <http://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-create.1.html?highlight=publish#publish-p-port>
> :
>
> podman --runtime /usr/bin/crun run \
>        --replace --pull always \
>        --label "io.containers.autoupdate=image" \
>        --tz 'Asia/Taipei' --cgroups no-conmon \
>        --restart=unless-stopped --name Sync \
>        -p "8888:8888" \
>        -p 55555 \
>        -v "/mnt/resilio_sync:/mnt/sync" \
>        -d resilio/sync
>
> podman port Sync
>
> + podman port Sync
> 8888/tcp -> 0.0.0.0:12385
> 55555/tcp -> 0.0.0.0:13589
>
> —

> .
>

> We recently merged some fixes to how ports are parsed. Can you try again against the later commit?

Yes, I can confirm this problem has been fixed in the master branch. Would you please consider releasing a hotfix version of the deb package on `https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers`?

`podman version`
```
ERRO[0000] The storage 'driver' option must be set in /etc/containers/storage.conf, guarantee proper operation.
Version:      3.0.0-dev
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.14.3
Git Commit:   915ae6d9bfa39515d7280f29b5d7d072753cc788
Built:        Thu Dec 17 06:35:06 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

We may do a 2.2.2 but I doubt it will arrive before the new year.
